compliance_patterns.description: Government authorities follow structured procurement with technical and financial evaluation
Authorities often have regulatory mandates that shape deliverable requirements

evaluator_insights.insight: Authority evaluators are often domain specialists with deep technical knowledge
evaluator_insights.implication: Superficial or generic proposals are quickly identified and scored down
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Demonstrate deep domain expertise with specific technical details and sector knowledge

evaluator_insights.insight: Authorities value innovation and international best practices as they shape sector policy
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals should include international benchmarks and innovative approaches
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include relevant international authority benchmarks and leading-practice examples

evaluator_insights.insight: Authorities often operate with greater autonomy and faster decision-making than ministries
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals can be more direct and outcome-focused, less protocol-heavy
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Focus on deliverable quality and measurable outcomes rather than ceremonial compliance

evaluator_insights.insight: Authorities frequently require consultants to interface with regulated entities
evaluator_insights.implication: Stakeholder management capability across the regulated ecosystem is valued
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Demonstrate experience managing multi-stakeholder engagements in regulated environments

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume authority processes are identical to ministry processes — authorities often have more flexible procurement
Do NOT assume the authority lacks technical expertise — many authorities employ senior domain specialists
Do NOT assume regulatory mandates are static — authorities frequently update their frameworks
